description: A secret or server-only value is read in a universal load and serialized into the client payload.
body: Failure mode: an API key or server-only datum is accessed in a +page.js universal load (or returned from a server load and forwarded wholesale), so it is serialized into the HTML and shipped to the browser where anyone can read it. Detection signals: a secret string appears in the page's initial data payload or network response; sensitive config is imported into a universal load; the server load returns more than the client needs. Do not place secrets in any code path whose output reaches the client. Safer fallback: read secrets only in +page.server.js, return to the client only the minimal non-sensitive fields, and verify the client payload contains no secret.
